CommissioIL 
P.O. BOX 801 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
C.A. Helms 
Pacific Telephone

San Francisco, California 94105 
Dear Mr. Helms:

blo_ent 
322-6441 
Stu_.llts of Economic Int .... st 
322-0U4 
This is in response to your letter dated April 7, 1981 in which 
you requested clarification regarding the qualification and 
filing obligations of a Major Donor Committee. 
A Major Donor committee as defined in Government Code Section 
820l3(c) is a committee which makes contributions totaling 
$5,000 or more in a calendar year. 
2 Cal. Admin. Code l8404(a) (copy enclosed) states that committees 
qualifying pursuant: to~overnment Code Section 820l3(c) terminate 
at the end of the calendar year in which the committee qualified. 
T.herefore, a Major Donor Committee does not incur any filing 
obligations until the committee requalifies in a calendar year. 
If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to contact me. 
Sincerely, 
~~A<,V/fn:~~~ 
Lynn Montgomery 
Political Reform Consultan~ 


April 7, 1981 
Ms. Lyn Montgomery 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 K Street 
Sacramento, CA. 
95814 
Dear Ms. Montgomery: 
Pacific Telephone 
140 New Montgomery Street 
San Francisco. California 94105 
Phone (415) 421-9000 
It is my understanding that Major Donor Committees do not incur any filing 
obligations until they "requalify" for major donor status in a specific 
year. 
To requalify as a Major Donor committee, contributions of $5,000 or 
more must be made. 
It would be helpful if you could clarify this issue for me. 
/t; /-. '" I 
/? 
\J .Lz·l ~C/"1~' 
C. A. Helms 
Accounting Staff Specialist

sections.question: You requested clarification regarding the qualification and filing obligations of a Major Donor Committee.
sections.conclusion: A Major Donor committee as defined in Government Code Section 82013(c) is a committee which makes contributions totaling $5,000 or more in a calendar year. 2 Cal. Admin. Code 18404(a) states that committees qualifying pursuant to Government Code Section 82013(c) terminate at the end of the calendar year in which the committee qualified. Therefore, a Major Donor Committee does not incur any filing obligations until the committee requalifies in a calendar year.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the qualification and filing obligations for a Major Donor Committee under California political reform law?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A Major Donor Committee qualifies by making contributions of $5,000 or more in a calendar year and automatically terminates at the end of that year. The committee has no filing obligations until it requalifies in a subsequent calendar year.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (April 7, 1981) and FPPC response (April 15, 1981). Response is from Lynn Montgomery, Political Reform Consultant. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION section headers present, but question and conclusion content clearly identifiable from letter body.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You requested clarification regarding the qualification and filing obligations of a Major Donor Committee.

CONCLUSION: A Major Donor committee as defined in Government Code Section 82013(c) is a committee which makes contributions totaling $5,000 or more in a calendar year. 2 Cal. Admin. Code 18404(a) states that committees qualifying pursuant to Government Code Section 82013(c) terminate at the end of the calendar year in which the committee qualified. Therefore, a Major Donor Committee does not incur any filing obligations until the committee requalifies in a calendar year.

embedding.summary: FPPC response to C.A. Helms of Pacific Telephone clarifying that Major Donor Committees, which contribute $5,000 or more annually, have no filing obligations outside of the calendar year in which they qualify, as they automatically terminate at year-end and must requalify to incur future obligations.
